evals.name: happy-path-yolo-add-feature
evals.prompt: yolo — add a /healthz endpoint to the API that returns 200 with `{ ok: true }`

assertions.text: yolo skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on the literal 'yolo' substring)
Phase 2 plan is posted IN-CHAT (no work-doc file created under docs/work/) and auto-passed — no sign-off prompt blocks the flow
Phase 2.5 spec-review still runs (3 parallel reviewers) — yolo does NOT skip the spec-review gate
Phase 5 multi-reviewer findings are auto-fixed in-place at every severity (Critical + Important + Minor) and a follow-up edit lands automatically; Minor findings additionally logged to chat
Phase 6 finish-menu is auto-passed: commit lands on current branch locally; no push to origin
Final commit message follows project convention and ends with the Claude Code Co-Authored-By trailer

evals.name: edge-go-full-auto
evals.prompt: go full auto — implement the rate-limit middleware we discussed

assertions.text: yolo skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'go full auto' substring)
Skill explicitly notes that yolo has NO pause/resume across sessions (no work-doc on disk)
Phase 4 verification triad (tests + lint + typecheck) runs with fresh evidence — yolo does NOT skip verification
If Phase 5 surfaces a Critical security finding, it is auto-fixed and the fix is itself re-verified before the commit lands
yolo skill is NOT invoked — the prompt 'do it' is too ambiguous and explicitly listed as a non-trigger in yolo's frontmatter description
Skill routing defaults to context-dependent behavior (continuing prior turn, or asking for clarification) rather than entering autopilot
Transcript does NOT auto-pass any gate based on the 'do it' phrase alone
